title: AI Engineer (Applied Scientist) III
company_focus: Healthcare financial platform and patient billing
description:
Our healthcare system is the leading cause of personal
bankruptcy in the U.S. Every year, over 50 million Americans
suffer adverse financial consequences as a result of seeking
care, from lower credit scores to garnished wages. The
challenge is only getting worse, as high deductible health
plans are the fastest growing plan design in the U.S.

Cedar’s mission is to leverage data science, smart product
design and personalization to make healthcare more
affordable and accessible. Today, healthcare providers still
engage with its consumers in a “one-size-fits-all” approach;
and Cedar is excited to leverage consumer best practices to
deliver a superior experience.

The Role:

Cedar is seeking an AI Engineer/Applied Scientist III to
join our team and help build and optimize product features
powered by generative AI. Our goal is to use AI to drive
bill clarity for patients and streamline workflows for our
customers, which are large healthcare systems across the US.

This role is for the Support/Kora squad, where you will work
on Kora, an AI Voice Agent purpose-built for patient
billing, which is the central product for our Cedar Support
offering. In this role, you will develop a deep
understanding of Cedar’s data and workflows and utilize your
skillset in rigorous data analysis combined with production-
level coding and rapid execution to design, build, and
improve the AI workflows that solve problems for patients
via the voice medium.

Responsibilities:

- Dive into unstructured data with finesse and rigor,
  combining quantitative and qualitative approaches to solve
  complex problems. This data can include call transcripts,
  patient account details, and more
- Design robust experiment plans that allow you to rapidly
  test hypotheses and solve problems that will lead to an
  improved patient experience (and execute these plans!)
- Build and enhance features for our AI Voice Agent, Kora –
  utilizing your skillset in production-level coding as well
  as prompt and context engineering
- Measure and track the impact of the new features and
  enhancements that you build, displaying a strong ownership
  mentality as you continue to iterate on Kora based on your
  results and findings
- Partner closely with other scientists, engineers, designers,
  and product counterparts to solve problems as a team
- Work in a fast-paced environment where we cheer each other
  on as we find small ways to make the patient experience a
  little bit better every day, while we work towards our
  ultimate goal to transform the healthcare financial
  experience

Skills and Experience:

- 3 or more years of full-time experience working in a
  structured product development environment on a product
  that had frequent releases (i.e., regular updates with
  enhancements, bug fixes, and new features)
- Experience collaborating with partners from different job
  functions, and the ability to translate business
  requirements into technical builds
- A strong understanding of both the scientific method and
  software engineering principles, with a proven ability to
  balance experimental rigor and rapid production deployment
- Experience with modern programming languages (in this role,
  you’ll use both Python and Typescript)
- Ability to thrive in an entrepreneurial environment, showing
  a tendency toward speed and action while achieving clarity
  from ambiguity
- A passion not only for AI, but also in the application of AI
  to solve real problems for real users. If you are a match
  for this role, you’ll feel comfortable diving deep into
  the data to figure out the best way to solve a particular
  user problem, presenting your findings, incorporating
  updates based on feedback, and implementing your solution
  in code. Ultimately doing all of this to make a tangible
  difference for users
- You are excited by the opportunity to use your combined
  skillset in data science and AI engineering to make a true
  impact on patient lives

Compensation Range and Benefits

- Salary/Hourly Rate Range*: $170,000 - $215,000
- This role is equity eligible
- This role offers a competitive benefits and wellness package

*Subject to location, experience, and education

What do we offer to the ideal candidate?

- A chance to improve the U.S. healthcare system at a high-
  growth company! Our leading healthcare financial platform
  is scaling rapidly, helping millions of patients per year
- Unless stated otherwise, most roles have flexibility to work
  from home or in the office, depending on what works best
  for you
- For exempt employees: Unlimited PTO for vacation, sick and
  mental health days–we encourage everyone to take at least
  20 days of vacation per year to ensure dedicated time to
  spend with loved ones, explore, rest and recharge
- 16 weeks paid parental leave with health benefits for all
  parents, plus flexible re-entry schedules for returning to
  work
- Diversity initiatives that encourage Cedarians to bring
  their whole selves to work, including three employee
  resource groups: be@cedar (for BIPOC-identifying Cedarians
  and their allies), Pridecones (for LGBTQIA+ Cedarians and
  their allies) and Cedar Women+ (for female-identifying
  Cedarians)
- Competitive pay, equity (for qualifying roles), and health
  benefits, including fertility & adoption assistance, that
  start on the first of the month following your start date
  (or on your start date if your start date coincides with
  the first of the month)
- Cedar matches 100% of your 401(k) contributions, up to 3% of
  your annual compensation
- Access to hands-on mentorship, employee and management
  coaching, and a team discretionary budget for learning and
  development resources to help you grow both professionally
  and personally

About us

Cedar was co-founded by Florian Otto and Arel Lidow in 2016
after a negative medical billing experience inspired them to
help improve our healthcare system. With a commitment to
solving billing and patient experience issues, Cedar has
become a leading healthcare technology company fueled by
remarkable growth. "Over the past several years, we've
raised more than $350 million in funding & have the active
support of Thrive and Andreessen Horowitz (a16z).

As of November 2024, Cedar is engaging with 26 million
patients annually and is on target to process $3.5 billion
in patient payments annually. Cedar partners with more than
55 leading healthcare providers and payers including
Highmark Inc., Allegheny Health Network, Novant Health,
Allina Health and Providence.
responsibilities: Analyze unstructured data including call transcripts and patient account details using quantitative and qualitative methods
Design and execute robust experiment plans to rapidly test hypotheses and improve patient experience
Build and enhance features for Kora AI Voice Agent using production-level coding and prompt/context engineering
Measure and track impact of new features, iterating based on results and findings with strong ownership
Collaborate with scientists, engineers, designers, and product teams to solve cross-functional problems
Work in a fast-paced environment to continuously improve the patient financial experience through AI solutions
use_cases: AI Voice Agent for patient billing inquiries and support
Bill clarity and financial communication for patients
Workflow automation for healthcare provider billing departments
Unstructured data analysis from call transcripts to improve patient interactions
Personalized patient financial experience using generative AI
Automated voice-based resolution of billing questions and payment discussions
ai_classification_reasoning:
This role involves building and optimizing Kora, an AI Voice
Agent for patient billing. The engineer will work directly
on prompt and context engineering, develop AI workflows, and
enhance features powered by generative AI, which is hands-on
AI development.
